items.title: FIFA World Cup: DHS secretary celebrates Iran's exit with ‘happy dance’
items.summary: Iran barely missed out on reaching ​the knockout rounds of the tournament; they drew all three of their matches in Group G and finished ‌as the ninth-best third-place team after Austria scored a last-minute goal against Algeria in the ​final group-stage match

items.title: Venezuela appreciates Indian team’s efforts to help earthquake victims
items.summary: The death toll from two powerful quakes in Venezuela crossed 1,700 on June 29, with thousands more injured and many more missing

items.title: Explosion in Monaco injuries 3 including Ukrainian tycoon, and suspected attacker flees to France
items.summary: The explosion occurred around 9 p.m. at the entryway of a residence near the French border, injuring two adults and a child who were taken to hospitals in France, Monaco’s Minister of State said

items.title: U.S.-India relationship at its lowest in 30 years: Indian-American Democrat leader
items.summary: Indian-American Congressman Ro Khanna claimed that during his recent visit to China, India’s Ambassador there had told him that a generation of trust was lost due to Mr. Trump’s policies

items.title: Reasonable to believe targeted killing of children part of larger plan for genocide of the Palestinian people, Justice Muralidhar says

items.title: Don't care about name on letterhead: Sergio Gor on Indo-Pacific command row

items.summary: From India’s Russian oil purchases to legal battles in Washington, the road to India-U.S. trade deal has become increasingly uncertain, and The Hindu explains why

items.title: Attempts to question FATF’s credibility often reflect fear of scrutiny: India at UN
items.summary: India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Parvathaneni Harish said that countries facing adverse assessments should address the identified deficiencies, strengthen domestic enforcement, improve financial transparency, and demonstrate irreversible action against terror-financing networks

items.title: Trump calls U.S. housing bill 'a big yawn,' presses voting curbs

items.title: Six killed in German ‘family tragedy’ shooting: police

items.title: Watch: CBSE Eases Three-Language policy for class 7, 8 and 9 | Above the Fold | 29.06.2026
items.summary: In this episode, we begin with a detailed discussion on CBSE’s proposed three-language policy; Europe’s worsening heatwave; key updates on Ram Temple developments, the Nasrapur minor rape and murder case, a Mumbai police arrest linked to poison capsules during a Muharram procession, protests over the Maharashtra TET paper leak, and the latest FIFA news updates.

items.title: One killed in shooting at site of California World Cup fan zone

items.title: Turkey ‘strongly condemns’ Israeli incursions in Syria

items.title: Israeli strike hits tent in central Gaza, killing three, including a child
items.summary: Israeli forces fatally shot a 15-year-old Palestinian teenager during a raid in the occupied West Bank on Monday (June 29), the Palestinian health ministry said, as violence surges in the Israeli-occupied territory.

items.title: Private channel suspended in Pakistan for 15 days over sectarian content
items.summary: The Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority on June 29 suspended with “immediate effect” Geo News’ licence for 15 days, saying the channel was “liable to hurt the religious sentiments of viewers”

items.title: Turkey must be ‘included’ in Europe security structures: Erdogan ahead of NATO summit
items.summary: Turkey's indispensable contributions to European security are sometimes overlooked; Turkey wanted ‘to participate in all defence and security initiatives’ on the continent, said Turkey President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

items.title: Europe’s deadly heatwave scorches eastern flank, takes aim at Ukraine
items.summary: This heatwave is the most severe ever recorded in Europe, and would have been "virtually impossible" this early in the summer without climate change, the World Weather Attribution group of scientists said.

items.title: Indian Army field hospital begins operations in quake-hit Venezuela
items.summary: “Indian Army doctors are taking care of people,” Mr. Jaiswal said, adding that the Venezuelan Vice-Health Minister and head of the Government of the Capital District of Venezuela visited the field hospital

items.title: Putin says Russia needs more air defence capability, tackling fuel issues
items.summary: The Russian President said he is expecting U.S. negotiators to come to Moscow once they are less busy with Iran

items.title: China imposes export controls on 40 Japanese entities as tensions with Tokyo rise
items.summary: Twenty Japanese entities, including multiple divisions of Mitsubishi Corporation, have been placed on a control list, which prohibits Chinese and foreign exporters from selling to them dual-use items made in China

items.title: Pakistan says carried out strikes along Afghanistan border, 29 militants killed

items.summary: The two sides anticipate meeting to work ‌out dispute over Strait of Hormuz; Hezbollah says reserves right to 'defend its homeland' after Israeli strikes on Lebanon

items.title: More than 1,300 excess deaths recorded in Europe heatwave: WHO
items.summary: At least 191 million people are forecast to endure temperatures of at least 35℃ on June 28, in Europe, with the heat particularly intense in Germany, the Czech Republic, Hungary and Poland, according to AFP estimates

items.title: Can neutral ships be lawfully attacked?
items.summary: What protections does international law afford neutral merchant vessels? When can neutral ships lawfully lose that protection? Can oil tankers and blockade runners be treated as military targets? What legal remedies are available to India after the deaths of its seafarers? How does international law regulate attacks?

items.title: Why was Trump’s H-1B fee struck down?
items.summary: What is the H-1B visa programme and who uses it? Why did the Trump administration impose the $100,000 fee? What impact was the policy expected to have on States and employers? Did the President have the authority to impose the fee? Why did the court classify the charge as a tax rather than a penalty?

items.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported ‌that Russia wanted to use Belarus as a springboard to step up ⁠attacks on Ukraine, and that Moscow was threatening to cut financial support if it did not agree

items.title: Ukraine's drone assault ignites major oil refinery in Russia
items.summary: Debris from downed Ukrainian drones sparked a blaze at the refinery in Slavyansk-na-Kubani, a town in Russia’s Krasnodar region, east of occupied Crimea, according to Gov. Veniamin Kondratyev

items.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait following U.S. strikes, threatens to end talks to end war
items.summary: An interim deal between the U.S. and Iran to end the war called for the Strait, which once saw a fifth of the world's oil and natural gas pass through it, to see transits resume

items.title: Iran warns ships not to bypass its chosen Hormuz route
items.summary: At the moment, Iran insists ships transiting the strait pass through a corridor close to its own shores, though this week dozens of vessels have travelled along the opposite side of the waterway, hugging the Omani coast

items.title: Russian attacks kill five in Ukraine, local officials say
